findings.location: s1 — 一括更新処理における更新チェック（主キーが組み合わせキーの場合）
findings.description: The `UsersExclusiveControl` class implementation example is entirely absent from the knowledge file. The source provides the complete code showing how to subclass `ExclusiveControlContext` for composite keys, which introduces four essential API methods that developers must call: `setTableName`, `setVersionColumnName`, `setPrimaryKeyColumnNames`, and `appendCondition`. Without this example, developers cannot implement composite-key exclusive control — they know to call `checkVersion`/`updateVersionWithCheck`, but not how to construct the required `ExclusiveControlContext` subclass.
findings.source_evidence: Source defines `UsersExclusiveControl` with: `setTableName("USERS")`, `setVersionColumnName("VERSION")`, `setPrimaryKeyColumnNames(PK.values())`, `appendCondition(PK.USER_ID, userId)`, etc. None of these methods appear anywhere in the knowledge file.

findings.location: s1 — 一括更新処理における更新チェック（主キーが組み合わせキーの場合）
findings.description: The note about framework-provided classes for composite-key bulk update — `WebView_CompositeKeyCheckboxTag`, `WebView_CompositeKeyRadioButtonTag`, and `nablarch.common.web.compositekey.CompositeKey` — is not mentioned in the knowledge file. These are presented as relevant supporting infrastructure for this use case.
findings.source_evidence: Source note: 「本フレームワークでは、:ref:`WebView_CompositeKeyCheckboxTag` と :ref:`WebView_CompositeKeyRadioButtonTag` 、nablarch.common.web.compositekey.CompositeKeyクラスを提供している。」

findings.description: `CommonExclusiveControlUtil` and `ExclusiveUserCondition` are PascalCase class names present in the s1 section content but are missing from the s1 hints array.
findings.source_evidence: `CommonExclusiveControlUtil` appears in the wrapper class code example included in s1. `ExclusiveUserCondition` appears in the `updateVersionWithCheck` loop code example in s1.
